desc: Architecture and process decisions @sheldon should default to. Captures structural patterns proven in the AIOSON framework itself. Extend after each architectural review.

nodes.title: workspace/template parity for agent files
nodes.s: Every change to .aioson/agents/*.md must be mirrored in template/.aioson/agents/*.md. The sync direction is template→workspace (sync:agents copies template to workspace), so new content must be propagated to template manually or it is silently lost on next sync.
nodes.p:
// after editing .aioson/agents/X.md, also Edit template/.aioson/agents/X.md
// verify with: diff -q .aioson/agents/X.md template/.aioson/agents/X.md
nodes.not: edit only the workspace copy — the template stays stale and the next sync wipes the change
nodes.warn: some pairs have legitimate preexisting diffs (e.g., qa.md feature:close logic) — review the diff before propagating, do not blindly mirror

nodes.title: classification gates scale process depth
nodes.s: MICRO/SMALL/MEDIUM controls delivery budget and evidence depth, not the canonical agent count or document chain. Tracked features use Product→Planner→Dev→QA; Sheldon and other specialists remain risk-triggered or explicitly requested detours, with the dossier as non-blocking context memory.
nodes.p:
1. Product owns one implementation-ready PRD
2. Planner owns one executable vertical plan
3. Dev implements through the normal entry point
4. QA performs proportional review
5. Classification changes budget and evidence depth only
nodes.not: running the full MEDIUM pipeline on every task because 'it's safer' — kills throughput on MICRO work
nodes.warn: do not let user urgency reclassify down — urgency does not shrink risk surface

nodes.title: independent evidence boundary for delivery reviewers
nodes.s: QA should read the PRD, implementation plan, prototype, changed production paths, and executable evidence while treating Dev claims and transcripts only as leads. Validator remains an optional specialist only when the approved plan deliberately defines a binary harness contract.
nodes.not: feeding the validator the full conversation history — it starts agreeing with prior agents instead of checking the binary contract
nodes.warn: classification alone never activates Validator or broadens QA into a long-running audit

nodes.title: repository discovery belongs inside executable planning
nodes.s: Planner must inspect the real repository, production entry point, nearest patterns, package versions, and tests before naming implementation paths. Discovery is an activity, not a mandatory document or agent hop; Architect is optional for one unresolved technical boundary.
nodes.p:
@product PRD → @planner inspects repository and writes one vertical plan → @dev implements → @qa reviews
Optional @architect answers one named boundary question and returns to Planner or Dev
nodes.not: generating discovery.md and architecture.md by default — parallel abstractions drift away from the PRD and repository
nodes.warn: brownfield discovery still matters, but its useful conclusions go into repository evidence, the plan, or the dossier

nodes.title: integrate via CLI command, not direct file write
nodes.s: When an agent needs to update non-final structured memory (dossier, project-pulse, features.md status), prefer its CLI command. Agents never auto-run feature:close, commit, or publish; those remain human gates after QA PASS.
nodes.src: AIOSON audit Lote B (discovery-design-doc, validator, tester dossier integration)
nodes.not: rewriting an artefact's whole structure to add one entry — breaks index sync and loses telemetry
nodes.warn: the CLI fallback path (manual edit) must remain available when CLI is missing — agents that hard-require CLI become unusable in plain Claude Code sessions
